varieties.name: Gijnlim
varieties.why: RHS AGM, all-male hybrid producing heavy yields of thick succulent spears from mid-April to June, quick to establish

varieties.name: Backlim
varieties.why: Vigorous all-male variety, produces immaculate straight spears with firm tips, excellent for white and green asparagus

varieties.name: Black Beauty
varieties.why: Classic reliable variety, heavy yields of glossy near-black skinned fruit, excellent for beginners

varieties.name: Galine F1
varieties.why: Professional-grade F1 hybrid, early and prolific, adapts well to UK climate, glossy dark fruit

varieties.name: Laurus nobilis
varieties.why: RHS AGM, the classic culinary bay tree, aromatic evergreen leaves for cooking, attractive year-round structure

varieties.name: Laurus nobilis (Standard)
varieties.why: Formally trained standard tree on clear stem, ideal for doorways and patios, same superb culinary leaves

varieties.name: Boltardy
varieties.why: RHS AGM, bolt resistant, early sowing, classic reliable variety

varieties.name: Chioggia
varieties.why: Italian heirloom, stunning candy-stripe interior, kids love slicing them

varieties.name: Loch Ness
varieties.why: RHS AGM, thornless, compact upright habit ideal for smaller gardens, heavy cropper up to 3.6kg per bush

varieties.name: Triple Crown
varieties.why: Thornless semi-erect canes, named for triple attributes of flavour, productivity and vigour, organic variety

varieties.name: Ben Connan
varieties.why: RHS AGM, compact bush ideal for smaller gardens, large sweet berries, excellent disease resistance, heavy yields

varieties.name: Summer Pearls Patio Black
varieties.why: Bred to reach just 1m high, perfect for small gardens and containers, heavy-cropping compact bush

varieties.name: Bluecrop
varieties.why: RHS AGM, the most reliable mid-season variety for UK gardens, abundant rich-flavoured fruit in August, good autumn colour

varieties.name: Duke
varieties.why: Early-season variety, firm medium-sized sweet berries from July, vigorous and productive

varieties.name: Aquadulce Claudia
varieties.why: RHS AGM, autumn sowing, very hardy, classic allotment variety

varieties.name: The Sutton
varieties.why: Compact dwarf broad bean, 30cm tall, no staking needed

varieties.name: Summer Purple
varieties.why: British-bred, no winter chilling needed, crops July to November with regular picking, good heat tolerance

varieties.name: Purple Rain F1
varieties.why: British-bred F1 hybrid, extended harvest from July to November, excellent vigour and uniformity

varieties.name: Brodie F1
varieties.why: Supermarket favourite for Christmas, mild flavour, outperforms Trafalgar in garden trials, reliable heavy yields

varieties.name: Marte F1
varieties.why: RHS AGM, heavy yields of large rounded sprouts well-spaced on stem for easy picking, crops October to Christmas

varieties.name: April (Spring)
varieties.why: Reliable spring cabbage, withstands bolting better than other varieties, solid pointed heads, autumn-sown for spring harvest

varieties.name: Marquess F1 (Sweetheart)
varieties.why: Autumn sweetheart type with tender sweet leaves, compact pointed heads ideal for smaller families

varieties.name: Nantes 2
varieties.why: Sweet cylindrical roots, deep root run suits open ground

varieties.name: Autumn King 2
varieties.why: RHS AGM, large maincrop, stores well, excellent for winter use

varieties.name: Rainbow Mix
varieties.why: Purple, yellow, white and orange roots, kids love pulling different colours

varieties.name: All The Year Round
varieties.why: One of the easiest cauliflowers to grow, large tight heads, suitable for successional sowings spring through summer

varieties.name: Boris F1
varieties.why: High-quality F1 hybrid, large dense white curds, strong wrapper leaves protect heads, good disease tolerance

varieties.name: Loretta
varieties.why: Premium self-blanching hybrid, vigorous upright growth, consistently uniform sticks, sweet and less stringy than other varieties

varieties.name: Blush
varieties.why: Outstanding self-blanching variety with unusual rose-tinged stems, refreshing crispy bite, beautiful in the garden

varieties.name: Jalapeño
varieties.why: Reliable outdoor variety in UK, medium heat, thick-walled, heavy yields

varieties.name: Hungarian Hot Wax
varieties.why: Prolific outdoors, long season, good for UK climate, medium heat

varieties.name: Black Beauty
varieties.why: Classic dark green, prolific cropper, bushy but needs 90cm spacing

varieties.name: F1 Defender
varieties.why: All-female F1, no bitter fruits, excellent disease resistance

varieties.name: Romanesco
varieties.why: Ribbed Italian variety, nutty flavour, ornamental flowers are edible

varieties.name: Marketmore
varieties.why: Reliable outdoor ridge variety, disease resistant, heavy cropper

varieties.name: Crystal Lemon
varieties.why: Heritage round yellow fruits, vigorous trailing, prolific outdoor cropper

varieties.name: Discovery
varieties.why: RHS AGM, early-season British-bred eating apple, crisp and juicy with strawberry flush, excellent disease resistance

varieties.name: Braeburn
varieties.why: Superb flavour, crisp texture, heavy cropper, stores well into winter, self-fertile

varieties.name: The Prince
varieties.why: RHS AGM, flat pods, heavy cropper, classic British allotment variety

varieties.name: Cobra
varieties.why: Climbing French bean, massive yields over long season, needs canes

varieties.name: Cambridge Favourite
varieties.why: RHS AGM, classic British variety, outstanding flavour, heavy cropper

varieties.name: Elsanta
varieties.why: Commercial standard, firm fruit, excellent disease resistance

varieties.name: Royal Sovereign
varieties.why: Heritage variety since 1892, unmatched flavour, RHS recommended

varieties.name: Florence
varieties.why: Classic Florence fennel for swollen bulb-like bases, delicate aniseed flavour, ferny foliage useful as herb garnish

varieties.name: Brown Turkey
varieties.why: The most reliable fig for UK gardens, fully hardy outdoors, heavy crops of sweet juicy figs with deep red flesh

varieties.name: Little Miss Figgy
varieties.why: Naturally dwarf patio variety, RHS Chelsea shortlisted, produces two crops per year, reaches only 1.8m

varieties.name: Solent Wight
varieties.why: UK bred, excellent for British climate, softneck type, stores well

varieties.name: Elephant Garlic
varieties.why: Giant mild cloves, kids amazed by the size, easy to peel

varieties.name: Invicta
varieties.why: RHS AGM, one of the heaviest-cropping gooseberries available, excellent mildew resistance, ideal for cooking and dessert

varieties.name: Hinnonmaki Red
varieties.why: Sweet red dessert gooseberry with aromatic flavour, highly productive, good resistance to mildew

varieties.name: Redbor
varieties.why: Tall ornamental purple kale, dramatic 90cm plants, heavy yields

varieties.name: Cavolo Nero (Nero di Toscana)
varieties.why: Italian heirloom, tall upright plant, superb flavour after frost

varieties.name: Musselburgh
varieties.why: Heritage Scottish variety, heavy crops of short thick stems, excellent flavour, exceptionally hardy through coldest winters

varieties.name: Northern Lights F1
varieties.why: British-bred maincrop with excellent winter hardiness, blue-green leaves turn stunning purple in cold, robust flavour

varieties.name: Iceberg (Webb's Wonderful)
varieties.why: Full-size crisphead, large hearts, classic British garden lettuce

varieties.name: All The Year Round
varieties.why: RHS AGM, butterhead, sow almost year-round, reliable hearting

varieties.name: Sturon
varieties.why: RHS AGM, excellent keeper, large round bulbs, reliable cropper

varieties.name: Red Baron
varieties.why: Deep red skin, mild sweet flavour, stores well, attractive in salads

varieties.name: Tender and True
varieties.why: RHS AGM, long smooth roots, canker resistant, excellent flavour after frost

varieties.name: Gladiator
varieties.why: F1 hybrid, smooth skin, canker resistant, good on heavier soils

varieties.name: Kelvedon Wonder
varieties.why: RHS AGM, first early, heavy cropper, 45cm height, good disease resistance

varieties.name: Alderman (Tall Telegraph)
varieties.why: Heritage tall variety 1.8m, huge pods, needs full support structure

varieties.name: Conference
varieties.why: RHS AGM, Britain's most popular pear, self-fertile, reliable heavy crops, juicy sweet flesh, stores well

varieties.name: Family Pear Tree
varieties.why: Three varieties grafted on one tree for cross-pollination and extended season, space-saving for small gardens

varieties.name: Perpetual Spinach
varieties.why: Huge yields in open ground, cut-and-come-again over full season

varieties.name: Giant Winter
varieties.why: Large leaves, excellent cold hardiness, overwinters well in open ground

varieties.name: Victoria
varieties.why: Britain's best-known plum, self-fertile, heavy crops of sweet egg-shaped fruits, ideal for eating fresh, pies and jams

varieties.name: Wonder Tree Victoria (Patio)
varieties.why: Naturally compact patio plum needing almost no pruning, same great Victoria flavour on a much smaller tree

varieties.name: Charlotte
varieties.why: RHS AGM, waxy salad potato, excellent flavour, reliable first early

varieties.name: Rocket
varieties.why: Fastest first early, 10-12 weeks to harvest, great for impatient kids

varieties.name: Maris Piper
varieties.why: Britain's favourite chip shop potato since the 1960s, dry floury texture, superb for roasting and mashing, reliable yields

varieties.name: Sarpo Mira
varieties.why: Unprecedented blight resistance, huge yields of tasty floury tubers, stores exceptionally well, ideal for organic growers

varieties.name: Jack O'Lantern
varieties.why: Classic carving pumpkin, 4-5kg fruits, kids love growing these for Halloween

varieties.name: Atlantic Giant
varieties.why: Competition variety, can reach 100kg+, ultimate fun for kids

varieties.name: Scarlet Globe
varieties.why: Classic round red, 25-day crop, vigorous grower in open ground

varieties.name: Mooli (April Cross)
varieties.why: Japanese daikon, long white roots, needs deep soil, mild sweet flavour

varieties.name: Glen Ample
varieties.why: RHS AGM, exceptional high-yielding mid-season variety, large deep red succulent berries, superb flavour, spine-free canes

varieties.name: Autumn Bliss
varieties.why: RHS AGM, one of the best autumn-fruiting varieties, large attractive red berries from late August to mid-October

varieties.name: Rovada
varieties.why: Exceptionally heavy cropper yielding up to 2.5kg per plant, long strings of large berries for easy harvesting, crops July-August

varieties.name: Jonkheer van Tets
varieties.why: Heavy-cropping early variety, large sweet berries, reliable and rewarding, excellent for jams, jellies and fresh eating

varieties.name: Victoria
varieties.why: Traditional heritage variety, excellent balance of sweetness and acidity, reliable heavy cropper year after year

varieties.name: Champagne
varieties.why: Early forcing variety producing long slender pink-tinged stalks, excellent flavour, ideal for early spring harvests

varieties.name: Cultivated Rocket
varieties.why: Fast-growing rows, succession sow every 3 weeks for continuous supply

varieties.name: Scarlet Emperor
varieties.why: Heritage classic, red flowers, heavy yields, needs 2m cane structure

varieties.name: Enorma
varieties.why: Exhibition variety, stringless 30cm pods, vigorous climbing habit

varieties.name: White Lisbon Winter Hardy
varieties.why: Overwinter sowing, very hardy, extends the season into early spring

varieties.name: Lilia
varieties.why: Red-skinned spring onion, mild sweet flavour, attractive in salads

varieties.name: Butternut Waltham
varieties.why: Classic butternut, stores 3+ months, sweet flesh, vigorous trailing

varieties.name: Crown Prince
varieties.why: Blue-grey skin, orange flesh, exceptional sweet flavour, stores well

varieties.name: Giant Single
varieties.why: Classic tall sunflower 2m+, huge seed heads, race to grow the tallest

varieties.name: Russian Giant
varieties.why: Can reach 3m+, edible seeds, traditional variety, kids love the scale

varieties.name: Sweet Genovese
varieties.why: Large-leaf Italian classic, best for pesto, full-size garden plant

varieties.name: Lettuce Leaf
varieties.why: Extra-large leaves up to 10cm, vigorous grower, needs garden space

varieties.name: Red King F1
varieties.why: Huge sweet-flavoured red peppers, wide blocky shape perfect for stuffing and roasting, heavy yields under glass

varieties.name: Thor F1
varieties.why: Produces impressive pods up to 25cm long, delicious sweet flavour, excellent for UK greenhouse or warm sheltered spot

varieties.name: Swift
varieties.why: Early F1, matures in UK climate, supersweet, reliable in Surrey

varieties.name: Lark
varieties.why: Supersweet F1, good cold tolerance, plant in blocks for wind pollination

varieties.name: Fordhook Giant
varieties.why: Large vigorous plant, heavy yields, thick white stems, needs space

varieties.name: Rhubarb Chard
varieties.why: Deep crimson stems, ornamental kitchen garden classic, vigorous

varieties.name: Gardener's Delight
varieties.why: RHS AGM, sweetest cherry tomato, indeterminate cordon, massive yields

varieties.name: Moneymaker
varieties.why: Heritage classic, reliable heavy cropper, cordon type, good disease tolerance

varieties.name: Sungold
varieties.why: RHS AGM, orange cherry, intensely sweet, incredibly productive

varieties.name: Sweetbell F1
varieties.why: Outstanding salad turnip, attractive purple tops with sweet succulent white flesh, can be eaten raw or cooked

varieties.name: Atlantic (Kew Collection)
varieties.why: Flat globe-shaped roots ideal as baby turnips, dark green erect leaves make excellent steamed greens, good for containers

varieties.name: Common Lemon Balm
varieties.why: Genuinely clay-tolerant fragrant herb — one of the few that thrives on heavy Surrey soil. Lemon-mint tea, bee magnet, practically indestructible.

varieties.name: All Gold
varieties.why: Golden-leaved ornamental variety. Slightly less vigorous but same lemon fragrance with better visual impact.

varieties.name: Corsican Mint
varieties.why: The best walk-on fragrance for Surrey clay. Thread-thin carpet between stepping stones, powerful peppermint scent when stepped on.

varieties.name: Gran's Favourite
varieties.why: RHS AGM. Old-fashioned clove-scented cottage garden flower. White with reddish-purple lacing. Edible flowers.

varieties.name: Mrs Sinkins
varieties.why: Heritage variety with legendary clove fragrance. White double flowers. Slightly untidy form but the scent is extraordinary.

varieties.name: Nicotiana sylvestris
varieties.why: RHS AGM. Towering 1.5m white trumpets, intensely jasmine-fragrant at dusk. One of the few fragrant annuals that loves clay soil.

varieties.name: Night-Scented Stock
varieties.why: Scatter seeds along paths and near seating. Unremarkable by day but at dusk releases one of the most intoxicating fragrances of any garden annual.

varieties.name: Double-Flowered
varieties.why: Apple-sweet scented flowers for chamomile tea. Traditional 'plant doctor' companion. Raised bed only on clay soil.

varieties.name: Treneague
varieties.why: Non-flowering ground cover with apple-sweet fragrance when walked on. For fragrant paths in raised beds.

varieties.name: Common Lemon Verbena
varieties.why: The most intensely citrus-scented plant you can grow. Electric lemon fragrance when leaves are rubbed. Outstanding herbal tea. Grow against warm south wall.

varieties.name: Attar of Roses
varieties.why: RHS AGM. Deep rose fragrance from foliage. Overwinter indoors. The most rewarding scented-leaf plant you can grow.
